model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the CLICK ME button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=70 dy=-30 conf=0.80 -> (254,396)
  iter 1: at (254,396) said dx=40 dy=10 conf=0.90 -> (294,406)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.content:
The previous iterations show the crosshair moving towards the target.
Iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=70 dy=-30 conf=0.80 -> (254,396)
Iter 1: at (254,396) said dx=40 dy=10 conf=0.90 -> (294,406)

The current crosshair is at (294,406). The target "CLICK ME" button appears to be around (274,401) according to the coordinates shown in the bottom right of the image.

To get from (294,406) to (274,401), the offset is:
dx = 274 - 294 = -20
dy = 401 - 406 = -5

The confidence can be high as the target is clearly visible and the crosshair is close.
